case.note: Case report PMC10492403 / PMID 37684617. 45 year old woman referred to Allergy and Immunology after anaphylaxis-type reaction with ibuprofen use. Underwent oral ASA challenge with reaction; passed acetaminophen and celecoxib challenges.

medications.source_span: She previously tolerated ibuprofen, naproxen, and acetaminophen and had no history of urticaria, angioedema, asthma, or nasal polyps.

An otherwise healthy 45 year old woman was referred to the Allergy and Immunology clinic after developing acute onset dyspnea, lip swelling, and generalized urticaria with ibuprofen use requiring treatment with intramuscular epinephrine in the emergency department.

An otherwise healthy 45 year old woman was referred to the Allergy and Immunology clinic after developing acute onset dyspnea, lip swelling, and generalized urticaria with ibuprofen use requiring treatment with intramuscular epinephrine in the emergency department.
On subsequent visits she passed treatment dose acetaminophen and celecoxib challenges.
On subsequent visits she passed treatment dose acetaminophen and celecoxib challenges.

report.patient_summary: Patient context received. Only verified findings are shown.

unverified_removed.claim_text: A 45 year old woman was referred to Allergy and Immunology after an acute reaction (dyspnea, lip swelling, generalized urticaria) following ibuprofen use, treated with intramuscular epinephrine. She subsequently underwent an oral aspirin challenge and passed acetaminophen and celecoxib challenges. The listed medications (aspirin, ibuprofen, epinephrine, acetaminophen, celecoxib) are documented as one-time diagnostic-challenge or acute-treatment agents rather than ongoing concurrent therapy.
unverified_removed.reason: Narrative reviewer: The summary asserts the patient underwent an oral aspirin challenge, but the patient context does not state this; the aspirin source span only references tolerance of ibuprofen, naproxen, and acetaminophen with no aspirin challenge described.; The claim that all listed medications are documented as one-time diagnostic-challenge or acute-treatment agents is not supported by the evidence, which is empty; medication statuses labeled 'one-time' are not corroborated by quoted evidence.

unverified_removed.claim_text: Are any of the listed agents (aspirin, ibuprofen, epinephrine, acetaminophen, celecoxib) intended as ongoing medications, or were they all one-time challenge or acute-treatment doses as the note indicates?
unverified_removed.reason: Narrative reviewer: Premise that the note indicates all agents were one-time challenge or acute-treatment doses is not supported by any quoted evidence (evidence is empty).

unverified_removed.claim_text: Should any allergy or intolerance documentation be updated in the record based on the challenge results described?
unverified_removed.reason: Narrative reviewer: The requested action to update allergy or intolerance documentation based on challenge results has no quoted evidence connecting the challenge results to a documentation update; evidence is empty.
